title: Lesson 21: LLVM in Practice — IR, Passes, Backends

questions.question: What does SSA (Static Single Assignment) mean in LLVM IR?
options: Each instruction can only be executed once
Each virtual register is assigned exactly once
Each function has a single return statement
Each basic block has a single predecessor
questions.explanation: In SSA form, every value (virtual register) is defined by exactly one instruction. When control flow merges and a variable has different values from different paths, a phi node is used to select the correct value.

questions.question: What does the `mem2reg` optimization pass do?
options: Moves stack allocations to heap memory
Promotes alloca/load/store operations to SSA phi-based registers
Merges multiple memory allocations into one
Converts pointer arithmetic to register arithmetic
questions.explanation: mem2reg promotes stack-allocated variables (alloca/load/store patterns) to SSA register form using phi nodes. This is typically the first pass run after Clang emits IR at -O0, since -O0 IR is full of explicit memory operations.

questions.question: Which LLVM tool is responsible for instruction selection, register allocation, and producing machine code?
options: opt
clang
llc
llvm-link
questions.explanation: llc is the LLVM static compiler that takes LLVM IR and compiles it to assembly or object code for a specific target. It handles instruction selection (via TableGen), scheduling, register allocation, and code emission.

questions.question: In the LLVM pass pipeline, what is the scope of a function pass?
options: It processes the entire LLVM module at once
It operates on individual loops within functions
It processes one function at a time, without seeing other functions
It only processes global variables
questions.explanation: Function passes operate on a single function at a time. They cannot see or modify other functions in the module. This restriction enables parallel execution of function passes and simplifies the pass framework.

questions.question: What is LLVM's role in the compilation ecosystem?
options: It is a specific programming language
It is a modular compiler infrastructure providing a target-independent IR, optimization passes, and code generation backends for multiple architectures
It is an operating system kernel
It is a debugger for C programs
questions.explanation: LLVM provides a common IR (LLVM IR) that frontends (Clang, Rust, Swift) target. Optimization passes transform the IR. Backends generate machine code for x86, ARM, RISC-V, etc. This modularity lets any language benefit from LLVM's world-class optimizations.
